Course Title: AI in Drug Discovery: Data Integrity Risks
Category: Healthcare & Life Sciences: Medical Services, Pharmacy, Biotechnology, Hospitals
Who is this for: Research scientists, data analysts, clinical researchers, regulatory affairs specialists, quality assurance managers, research coordinators, biostatisticians, pharmaceutical researchers
Team or Dept this is for: Research and Development, Quality Assurance, Regulatory Affairs, Data Management, Clinical Research, Compliance
Description: How flawed data can impact research outcomes.
Prerequisites: A basic familiarity with scientific research concepts and an interest in how data quality affects drug development outcomes are all that is needed to get started with this course.
Learning objectives: By the end of this course, you will be able to identify the key data integrity risks associated with AI-driven drug discovery and explain how flawed or biased data can compromise research outcomes. You will also be able to apply practical strategies to recognise and mitigate data quality issues within healthcare and life sciences research programmes.
